steps.title: Identify the antecedent
steps.content: The blank fills the subject slot of "_____ will begin to combust." The previous sentence names cottonseed oil — a singular, non-human noun — as the thing that can't absorb more heat. The pronoun must refer back to it.

steps.title: Apply pronoun-antecedent agreement — per-choice diagnosis
steps.content:
The pronoun must agree with its antecedent in number (singular) and be grammatically appropriate for a non-human referent.
  • Choice A — "they": plural pronoun. Cottonseed oil is singular, so "they" creates a number mismatch. Eliminated.
  • Choice B — "that": a demonstrative adjective or relative pronoun, not a personal pronoun that can stand alone as a subject here without a following noun. Eliminated.
  • Choice C — "those": plural demonstrative pronoun. Same number mismatch as "they." Eliminated.
  • Choice D — "it": singular, third-person pronoun for non-human referents. Correctly refers back to the singular cottonseed oil. Correct.
